title: Kubernetes HPA Autoscaling Failure During Traffic Surge
symptoms: Frontend pod CPU at 95% across all 3 replicas (HPA minimum)
HPA showing 'unable to fetch metrics' in describe output
metrics-server pod in CrashLoopBackOff from earlier OOM
Request timeout rate at 65% on frontend endpoints
Kubernetes Events showing HPA unable to compute desired replicas
root_cause: Metrics-server pod OOMKilled 2 hours prior, leaving HPA without CPU metrics to trigger autoscaling during the traffic surge from a marketing campaign launch
resolution: Restarted metrics-server with increased memory allocation, manually scaled frontend to 20 replicas while HPA recovered, and implemented metrics-server monitoring
preventive_actions: Implement metrics-server health monitoring with immediate alerting
Increase metrics-server memory allocation with proper resource requests
Add HPA fallback using custom metrics from Prometheus as secondary source
Implement manual scaling alerts when HPA is unable to function
Create pre-campaign scaling runbook with manual scale-up checklist

timeline.event: Frontend pods at 95% CPU, HPA not scaling
P1 alert fires, Alex Johnson investigates
HPA unable to fetch metrics, metrics-server down discovered

embedding_summary: Kubernetes HPA autoscaling failure during marketing campaign traffic surge due to metrics-server being OOMKilled 2 hours earlier. Frontend stuck at 3 pods instead of scaling to 20+. 42,000 users impacted with 65% timeout rate. Resolved by restarting metrics-server and manual scaling.
